subject: mint_event (ponder.mint_event) — GeneralMints contract 0x048327A187b944ddac61c6e202BfccD20d17c008 (mibera_vm/mibera-shadow) + 0x230945E0... (mibera_gif)
claim: mint_event is a silent live gap — frozen-at-boundary forward AND (contract active OR a live consumer reads it) → needs a forward handler ported.
ground_truth: Registered-but-unsubscribed: ponder.config.mibera.ts:218 registers GeneralMints (startBlock 21424739) so ponder FETCHES its logs, but NO handler subscribes (zero ponder.on('GeneralMints...'), no mints.ts/vm-minted.ts ported) → events discarded. Live proof: on-chain mint at block 21508459 tokenId 3245 (tx 0x304439b9..., ~83,720 blocks past boundary) is absent from vRR1 (query=0 rows); vRR1 mint_event has 0 rows at/after boundary (max_block 21423803, mibera_vm max token 3241). Dead consumers: pending_emits=0 (NATS publishMintEvent collectionSlug 'mibera-shadow' never enqueues → Ruggy gets no announcements) AND gateway exposes a live MintEvent field reading the same frozen table. Appendix-C 'contract inactive' assumption is falsified.
refutation_attempted: Defaulted to refuted and attacked 4 ways: (1) 'has a handler' — re-grepped: no GeneralMints handler file, zero ponder.on('GeneralMints...') subscriptions, index.ts registers 12 modules none for mints, zero mint_event writes; only mibera-shadow refs are a test + an UNUSED type-union member in nats-publisher.ts. (2) 'advancing-live' — vRR1 mint_event = 0 rows past boundary 21424739, max_block 21423803, while berachain checkpoint scans to ~21,529,488. (3) 'contract inactive → frozen-only accepted' — on-chain eth_getLogs found a real mint (Transfer from 0x0) at block 21508459 token 3245, ABSENT from vRR1; mibera_vm DB max token 3241 vs chain 3245. (4) 'no live consumer' — pending_emits count=0 (NATS/Ruggy path dead), gateway also exposes live MintEvent root field on the frozen table. All four refutations failed.
recommended_action: Port the envio handler chain to ponder: create ponder-runtime/src/handlers/general-mints.ts implementing ponder.on('GeneralMints:Transfer') (mints.ts:75 context.MintEvent.set + publishSlug) and ponder.on('GeneralMints:Minted') (vm-minted.ts:37 encodedTraits set + vm-minted.ts:55 publishMintEvent collectionSlug 'mibera-shadow'/'mibera-gif'), enqueueing to pending_emits gated by isLiveEvent so OutboxFlushBera drains to NATS. Add the import to ponder-runtime/src/index.ts. Backfill the boundary→head gap (mibera_vm tokens 3242-3245+ and mibera_gif) so the ~4+ missed mints land and Ruggy fires. Route this through /implement (new ponder handler = schema/contract interaction, NOT a micro-fix). Re-grade the other 7 Appendix-C deferrals with the same registered-but-unsubscribed + on-chain-activity + consumer-path triad.
learning: For B-1: 'frozen-only is fine' is ONLY safe when the contract is genuinely inactive AND no live consumer reads the entity. Verify BOTH against the territory, not the spec. Critical pattern to grep for: a contract REGISTERED in ponder.config (startBlock=boundary) but with NO matching ponder.on() subscription/handler module — this is 'registered-but-unsubscribed', which looks wired but silently drops events. Cross-check each Appendix-C deferred entity with (a) eth_getLogs for a post-boundary event proving on-chain activity, (b) a vRR1 count of rows at/after the boundary block, and (c) presence of a consumer path (pending_emits enqueue for NATS, or a live gateway root field). A type-union member or test fixture referencing the slug is NOT a handler — only a real ponder.on subscription that writes the table + enqueues emits counts.

subject: badge_holder (ponder.badge_holder)
claim: badge_holder is a silent live gap — frozen-at-boundary forward AND a live consumer reads it → needs a CubBadges1155 forward handler ported.
ground_truth: Independently re-verified live. NO forward handler: zero badgeHolder writers in ponder-runtime/src/; CubBadges1155 only in ponder.config.mibera.ts:203-208 (Erc1155Abi, startBlock 21424739, no ponder.on); envio has it at src/handlers/badges1155.ts:198 context.BadgeHolder.set via CubBadges1155.TransferSingle(:254)/TransferBatch(:296), contract 0x574617ab9788e614b3eb3f7bd61334720d9e1aac. FROZEN: vRR1 ponder.badge_holder n=8010, max(updated_at)=1777063492=2026-04-24T20:44:52Z, single-row tail (c=1); badge_balance/badge_amount n=435037 each, identical frozen max_ts; peer mibera_transfer advances to block 21527940/ts 1780070596=2026-05-29T16:03:16Z; green indexer live at bera ~21529488 ~30ms/block. LIVE consumer: gateway returns BadgeHolder rows live; cubquests getBadgeHoldersFromEnvio called from production trigger job daily-quest-badge-snapshot:89 + generator.ts:111. Probe nuance corrected: probe cited mibera_transfer/action updated_at columns that do not exist (only badge_* tables have updated_at); re-derived advancing-peer signature from mibera_transfer.timestamp/block_number — same conclusion. Distinct from mint_event: no NATS/outbox path for badges, so Ruggy unaffected; impact is stale GraphQL reads feeding a daily snapshot job.
refutation_attempted: Tried three refutations, all failed. (a) 'It has a forward handler' → refuted: grep in ponder-runtime/src/ returns zero badgeHolder writers; CubBadges1155 is in ponder.config.mibera.ts:203-208 but registered with NO ponder.on handler; index.ts imports 12 modules, none touch badges. (b) 'data_state=advancing-live (strong refutation)' → refuted: badge_holder max(updated_at)=2026-04-24 is a single-row frozen tail, while same-DB peer mibera_transfer advances to 2026-05-29 16:03 and green indexer is live at bera ~21529488 — badge is ~5 weeks behind, zero rows after boundary. (c) 'contract inactive AND no live consumer → accepted frozen-only (Sprint-M outcome, severity none/low)' → refuted on the consumer leg: cubquests getBadgeHoldersFromEnvio is wired into a live production scheduled job (src/trigger/daily-quest-badge-snapshot.ts:89) and lib/badge-snapshot/generator.ts:111, not dead code, and the gateway serves BadgeHolder rows live. The disjunction needs only one leg; the live-consumer leg holds even though contract_active_onchain stays unverified.
recommended_action: Port CubBadges1155 forward handler from envio to ponder: add ponder.on("CubBadges1155:TransferSingle") and ponder.on("CubBadges1155:TransferBatch") writing badge_holder + badge_balance + badge_amount (mirror src/handlers/badges1155.ts), register the module in ponder-runtime/src/index.ts, and backfill the ~5-week gap (2026-04-24 → live). No NATS/outbox wiring needed (unlike mint_event — badges have no publishMintEvent path). Verify post-port that ponder.badge_holder max(updated_at) advances past 1777063492 and gateway BadgeHolder rows reflect post-boundary transfers; notify the cubquests owner that daily-quest-badge-snapshot has been reading 5-week-stale holder data since the migration boundary. Optionally confirm contract activity via RPC eth_getLogs on 0x574617ab TransferSingle since block 21424739 to size the backfill.
learning: For Appendix-C deferred entities, the 'inactive contract' assumption is NOT the load-bearing test — the live-consumer leg is. When contract_active_onchain is unverifiable from inside ponder (no handler = no events ever land, so the DB can't prove activity), a real gap is still forced the moment a production consumer reads the frozen table. Verify consumers by tracing the GraphQL field to an actual call site on a live path (scheduled job / API route), not just to a query helper that could be dead code — here it was a Trigger.dev daily snapshot job. The frozen-snapshot signature is reliably: single-row updated_at tail + identical max_ts across companion tables + a sibling table in the SAME DB advancing to live head. Watch for probe evidence citing timestamp columns the peer tables don't actually have (mibera_transfer has no updated_at) — re-derive from real columns before trusting comparisons. Fast triage rule: handler-less-but-registered contract in ponder.config (abi present, no ponder.on) = guaranteed frozen-forward; pair with one confirmed live read = port the handler.

subject: badge_amount (ponder.badge_amount)
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
ground_truth: Three independently re-verified facts force refuted: (a) On-chain — eth_getLogs scan of CubBadges1155 0x574617ab9788e614b3eb3f7bd61334720d9e1aac across the FULL boundary→head window (21424739→21529516, 104,777 blocks) returned 0 events; contract is a deployed-but-dormant minimal proxy. (b) Data state — vRR1 ponder.badge_amount frozen at max(updated_at)=1777063492 (2026-04-24) with 435,037 rows, while live baseline ponder.mibera_transfer is at max_ts=1780070596 (now) and berachain checkpoint is at block ~21529514 — proves the indexer is at head while badge_amount sits 35 days stale = frozen-at-boundary, not advancing. (c) Consumer — gateway serves BadgeAmount (435,037 rows confirmed live) but NO ecosystem repo queries BadgeAmount/badgesHeld; the only CubBadges-referencing repos (score-mibera, mibera-dimensions) query the Action/hold1155 entity against the OLD hyperindex endpoint, never belt-gateway's BadgeAmount. frozen-at-boundary + no forward handler + inactive contract + no live consumer = exactly Sprint-M Appendix-C accepted "frozen-only" outcome.
refutation_attempted: Tried all three refutation routes and the confirm route. (1) "It HAS a forward handler" — re-ran grep -rni badge across ponder-runtime/src (ZERO hits), confirmed no badges1155.ts file, confirmed index.ts imports only 11 modules with no badges1155 import. data_state is NOT advancing-live: vRR1 badge_amount max(updated_at)=1777063492 (2026-04-24) is pinned at the boundary while live baseline mibera_transfer max_ts=1780070596 (2026-05-29, now) and the berachain checkpoint sits at block ~21529514 (head 21529516), ~104,777 blocks past boundary 21424739. So forward_handler_exists=no AND data_state=frozen-at-boundary — both confirmed. (2) "Contract active on-chain" — independently scanned eth_getLogs for CubBadges1155 0x574617ab... across all 104,777 blocks from boundary to head: 0 Transfer events. eth_getCode returns a deployed minimal-proxy (length ~328) but it is dormant (no event emissions). Considered the proxy-upgrade angle (a proxy COULD be re-pointed), but rejected it as speculative — the grading criterion is active-NOW/emitting-NOW, and the chain shows zero activity. (3) "A live consumer reads it" — gateway DOES serve BadgeAmount (435,037 frozen rows, max updatedAt 2026-04-24 confirmed via live query), but ecosystem grep for PascalCase BadgeAmount/badgesHeld root field across score-mibera, freeside-characters, mibera-dimensions found ZERO consumers. The two repos referencing the CubBadges address query the Action entity (actionType:"hold1155"), NOT BadgeAmount, and both default to the OLD endpoint indexer.hyperindex.xyz/914708e (not belt-gateway). NATS/emit path: badges1155.ts has zero pending_emits/publishMintEvent calls — never on the Ruggy emit path. Confirm route fails: contract_active=inactive AND consumer_impact=none, so the AND-conditions for "real" are not met.
recommended_action: Keep deferred — do NOT port a badges1155 forward handler in the B-1 run. badge_amount is correctly frozen-only per Sprint-M Appendix C: dormant contract (0 events in 104,777 blocks) + zero live consumers of the migrated BadgeAmount entity. The frozen 435,037 rows remain queryable for any historical reads. Two follow-ups: (1) Add a cheap on-chain liveness tripwire — if CubBadges1155 0x574617ab... ever emits a TransferSingle/TransferBatch past the boundary, the deferral inverts and the handler must be ported; a periodic eth_getLogs probe would catch a proxy-upgrade reactivation. (2) Separately grade the Action/hold1155 entity for this same contract as its own ledger row — it is identically frozen and is the path actual consumers (mibera-dimensions) query, albeit against the old endpoint today.
learning: Frozen-at-boundary + no-forward-handler is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for a silent live gap — it is the DEFAULT accepted state for every Appendix-C deferral. The gap is only "real" when paired with a liveness driver: contract emitting events past the boundary OR a consumer reading the migrated entity off belt-gateway. Two specific refutation shortcuts proved decisive and reusable: (1) Same-entity-name on the gateway does NOT imply a consumer — grep the PascalCase ROOT FIELD (e.g. BadgeAmount), not the contract address; repos often touch the same contract via a DIFFERENT entity (Action/hold1155 here) and against the OLD hyperindex endpoint, so the migrated entity is genuinely orphaned. (2) Confirm dormancy by eth_getLogs across the whole boundary→head window, not just eth_getCode — a deployed proxy (length ~328) can look "alive" but emit nothing. Also flag for separate grading: the Action/hold1155 path is the IDENTICAL twin deferral (same contract, same 435,037 rows, same frozen ts) — grade it as its own row, do not assume this row covers it.

subject: badge_balance (ponder.badge_balance)
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
ground_truth: Verified: forward_handler_exists=no AND data_state=frozen-at-boundary are both TRUE, but the decisive third condition fails -- contract_active_onchain=inactive (0 logs of any topic across 105k post-boundary blocks, method-validated by positive control) AND no consumer divergence (the gateway serves a static-but-correct snapshot of a dormant collection; the live mibera-dimensions consumer points at envio HyperIndex by default anyway). Frozen-only for an inactive contract is precisely Sprint-M Appendix C's accepted outcome, not a silent live gap.
recommended_action: Keep badge_balance in the 'accept-frozen-only' bucket for B-1 -- do NOT port a forward handler now (no active emission, no diverging consumer). Record it as LATENT HARDENING: if CubBadges1155 (0x574617ab...) resumes minting/transfers post-boundary, ponder will silently under-report holdings (it does not even fetch logs for the handler-less registered contract), so set a lightweight watch -- a periodic on-chain log-count check on CubBadges past the boundary; flip to 'port handler' the moment count > 0. The port itself is mechanical: lift src/handlers/badges1155.ts (TransferSingle/TransferBatch -> adjustBadgeBalances -> BadgeBalance.set) into a ponder handler and add the import to ponder-runtime/src/index.ts. Prioritize the genuinely-active gaps first (e.g. mint_event / GeneralMints) over this dormant one.
learning: A frozen-at-boundary table with NO forward handler is only a silent LIVE gap when the underlying contract is actually producing logs past the boundary OR a live consumer's reads diverge from chain truth. The cheap, decisive discriminator is a method-validated on-chain log scan (eth_getLogs any-topic over the full post-boundary window WITH a positive control on a known-active contract). If post-boundary logs == 0, the frozen snapshot still equals chain truth and the gap is latent hardening, not bleeding -- exactly Sprint-M's accepted 'accept-frozen-only' outcome. Contrast with mint_event: GeneralMints emits past the boundary, so its missing handler IS an actively-bleeding gap (no NATS emit -> Ruggy misses announcements). Also verify the consumer's EFFECTIVE endpoint: a consumer that defaults to the envio HyperIndex URL (not the ponder gateway) is not even reading the migrated frozen table. Default to refuted; let the territory force 'real'.

subject: bgt_boost_event
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
ground_truth: Real silent live gap: no forward handler + frozen-at-boundary table + active contract + live consumer reading the frozen data.
refutation_attempted: Defaulted to refuted and pursued all three refutation paths. (1) 'It has a forward handler' — FAILED: re-grepped ponder-runtime/src, zero references; the 35 ponder.on registrations contain no BGT/QueueBoost/boost; table defined but never written. (2) 'data_state is advancing-live' — FAILED: vRR1 table hard-frozen at max_block 21,423,939 with 0 rows at/above the boundary, while the runtime checkpoint for the same chain (berachain) is at 21,529,534; frozen, not advancing. (3) 'contract inactive AND no live consumer → frozen-only = Sprint-M accepted outcome' (the strongest path) — FAILED on both legs: contract is active (59 QueueBoost logs above boundary via independent public Berachain RPC, not ponder's own store), and a live consumer reads it (belt-gateway points CURRENT_BELT_DATABASE_URL at vRR1 and serves BgtBoostEvent over GraphQL with the frozen row + count 1,474,967). Also sanity-checked the two probe-flagged red herrings (empty ponder_sync.logs = handler-absence artifact; blue Postgres-3vIC = frozen migration snapshot) and confirmed neither weakens the activity proof. Territory forces 'real'.
recommended_action: B-1: port the envio handler at src/handlers/bgt.ts:42 (handleBgtQueueBoost = BgtToken.QueueBoost.handler) into a new ponder-runtime/src/handlers/bgt.ts that ponder.on('BgtToken:QueueBoost') and writes bgt_boost_event via context.db.insert (plus the parallel recordAction 'delegate' write into ponder.action, since that slice is also partially frozen at 1,474,967 rows while action overall advances — a deceptive partial freeze). Register it in ponder-runtime/src/index.ts (15th handler). Contract is already registered in ponder.config.mibera.ts:196-201 and table already exists at ponder.schema.ts:65, so no schema/config change is needed — handler-only. No NATS wiring required (envio source does not emit). After porting, backfill the boundary gap (blocks 21,424,739..head) so the ~105k-block hole closes; verify vRR1 rows>=boundary becomes nonzero and gateway BgtBoostEvent newest blockNumber advances past 21,423,939. Appendix C's 'accept-frozen-only' deferral for this entity was based on a wrong 'inactive contract' assumption and should be corrected in the migration-A spec.
learning: A frozen table whose contract is independently confirmed active on-chain past the boundary AND whose data is served to a live consumer is a REAL silent gap even when there is NO NATS/announcement symptom. Absence of an alarm (no publishMintEvent, no pending_emits) is what makes it silent, not what makes it safe — it is the opposite of refuting evidence. When grading, always source contract-activity from an INDEPENDENT witness (public RPC), never from ponder's own sync store: a missing handler blanks ponder_sync.logs for that contract, which would falsely read as 'inactive' and refute a real gap. Likewise the blue migration-source DB (Postgres-3vIC, _meta frozen at the boundary) is never a liveness witness. The decisive contradiction is runtime-checkpoint-advancing (21,529,534) vs table-frozen (21,423,939) on the same chain — that gap, plus active contract plus live consumer, forces 'real'.

subject: candies_inventory (ponder.candies_inventory)
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
ground_truth: Refuted: frozen-forward is TRUE but both disjuncts of the gap-clause fail (contract dormant on-chain AND no live consumer), so the absent ponder handler produces no missing live data and no broken consumer — exactly Sprint-M's accepted accept-frozen-only outcome.
refutation_attempted: Attempted to refute the refutation (i.e. force 'real') three ways: (1) re-grepped ponder-runtime for any candies forward-write — zero; (2) checked whether the contract is active by segmenting events above the boundary on the MIGRATED db AND independently on the LIVE-at-head blue source that watches the contract from genesis — both zero above boundary, green head ~189k blocks beyond last mint; (3) hunted a live consumer across 3 consumer repos + NATS emit path + gateway query usage — none. Also verified the contract IS registered in ponder.config (so a handler gap would matter IF active), and that Score's real candies_minter source is a different table. Could not force 'real'.
recommended_action: Mark candies_inventory CLOSED on the B-1 ledger as a confirmed correct accept-frozen-only deferral (Appendix C). Do NOT port a forward handler — it would index nothing (dormant contract) and feed no consumer. Optional hygiene: annotate Appendix C with the CORRECT rationale (dormant contract + zero consumers) rather than the assumed 'inactive contract', and add a guard note that CandiesMarket1155 is registered in ponder.config.mibera.ts with no handler so a future re-activation of 0xeca035.../0x80283f... would silently drop events — add a low-priority watch (alert if erc1155_mint_event[mibera_drugs] block_number ever exceeds 21424739) rather than building the handler now.
learning: Two failure modes are NOT silent live gaps even when forward_handler=no AND frozen-at-boundary: a DORMANT contract (segment events strictly above the boundary block on a live-at-head source; if 0 and head is far past, it's dormant) and a CONSUMER-LESS entity (cross-repo grep + NATS-path check + gateway-query check all empty). 'Registered in ponder.config but unhandled' is a trap that only matters if the contract is active. Always cross-check the migrated db against the live blue/3vIC source — agreement on above-boundary=0 is decisive proof of dormancy, not just a missing handler. Confirm the entity's REAL consumer table (Score read candies via Action/CandiesBacking, not candies_inventory) before crediting impact. The mint_event/GeneralMints instance that bit differs precisely because it was active AND on the NATS path.

subject: candies_backing
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
ground_truth: Three axes independently re-verified. (1) NO forward handler: 31 ponder.on regs / 14 modules, zero candies; envio mints1155.ts:73,160 never ported. (2) FROZEN: vRR1 candies_backing n=1108 max 2026-05-25T07:46:14Z, 0 post-cutover. (3) NOT LAG: berachain checkpoint head=NOW-17s, control mibera_transfer advanced today; CandiesMarket1155 logs fetched then discarded. (4) LIVE CONSUMER: score-mibera computed.handler.ts:762-792 credits candies_contributions; HIDDEN_EVENT_CATEGORIES is feed-display-only.
refutation_attempted: Tried all four refutation routes and each FAILED. (a) 'HAS a forward handler' — re-grepped 31 ponder.on regs + full candies grep across ponder-runtime/src, zero. (b) 'data advancing-live' — vRR1 shows 0 rows after cutover, definitively frozen. (c) 'just indexer lag' — checkpoint head decodes to NOW-17s and handler-backed control table advanced to today, so freeze is handler-caused. (d) 'contract inactive AND no consumer -> frozen-only is Sprint-M's accepted outcome' — failed on consumer axis: Score's computed.handler reads candies_backing into score credit (verified file:line), so the disjunction (active OR consumer) is satisfied by the consumer alone even though contract-activity stays genuinely unknown (blue envio frozen at boundary, internal RPC unreachable).
recommended_action: Port envio src/handlers/mints1155.ts handleCandiesMintSingle/Batch into a new ponder-runtime/src/handlers/candies-market1155.ts registering ponder.on('CandiesMarket1155:TransferSingle') and ('CandiesMarket1155:TransferBatch'), writing candies_backing (+candies_inventory, erc1155_mint_event, mibera_order) for collectionKey==='mibera_drugs' when tx.value>0 — mirror the already-ported PuruApiculture1155/MiberaSets ERC-1155 handler shape (puru-apiculture1155.ts is the closest template). Add the import to ponder-runtime/src/index.ts. Contract is already registered (config.mibera.ts:210, startBlock 21424739) so a reindex from boundary will backfill the 2026-05-25->now gap. No NATS path needed (mints1155 has no publishMintEvent). After deploy, re-verify candies_backing advances past cutover on vRR1 and Score candies_contributions credit resumes. Reclassify in the B-1 green-belt run: Appendix-C 'accept-frozen-only' is incorrect for this entity.
learning: A registered-but-unhandled contract is the silent-gap signature: the indexer fetches the logs (contract in ponder.config startBlock) and the schema table exists, but with no ponder.on(...) registration the events are silently discarded — there is NO error, NO log, just a frozen table that looks identical to a dormant contract. To distinguish handler-gap from genuine inactivity, decode the chain's _ponder_checkpoint head and compare to a handler-backed control table on the SAME chain; if the control advances to NOW but the suspect table is frozen at the migration boundary, the freeze is handler-caused. Crucially, the Appendix-C 'contract inactive -> accept-frozen-only' deferral is independently falsifiable on the CONSUMER axis alone: even if on-chain activity is unprovable (frozen blue oracle + unreachable internal RPC), a live consumer that reads the entity (here Score's candies_contributions credit) forces 'real' regardless of contract status. Also: a feed-hide (HIDDEN_EVENT_CATEGORIES) is NOT a consumer-drop — always trace whether the score/compute path still reads the 'hidden' category before discounting consumer impact.

subject: mibera_order (ponder.mibera_order)
claim: frozen-only forward gap with active contract / live consumer
recommended_action: Do NOT prioritize porting a forward handler for B-1. This is exactly Sprint-M's accepted Appendix-C "frozen-only / inactive contract" outcome, and even weaker — the source never produced an order row. No consumer is being starved of previously-flowing data (distinct from mint_event, which starved Ruggy's live NATS announcements). OPTIONAL future-proofing (severity low, not a B-1 blocker): port the CandiesMarket1155 TransferSingle/TransferBatch -> miberaOrder handler into ponder-runtime/src/handlers/ and register ponder.on("CandiesMarket1155:TransferSingle"/"TransferBatch") so that IF the SilkRoad marketplace resumes non-mint transfer activity above block 21424739, orders index forward and the existing mibera-interface/honeyroad consumers light up automatically. Add a monitor: alert if ponder.erc1155_mint_event for collection_key in ('mibera_drugs','mibera_candies') advances past 21424739 — that's the trigger to actually port the handler.
learning: Two independent facts must both hold before grading "real": (a) data_state must be frozen-at-boundary WITH rows — verify by checking BOTH the ponder target AND the envio SOURCE all-time count; a table that the source never populated (source count=0 ever) cannot be a "silent live gap" no matter how many consumers query it, because nothing was ever flowing to starve. (b) Contract activity must be checked by comparing the same-contract event's max(block_number) against the migration boundary AND against a known-live handler's advancing block — if the contract's blocks sit below the boundary while live handlers advance, the contract is frozen/inactive and a missing handler drops nothing. Also: when a probe greps a scoped subdir (ponder-runtime/) and cites high line numbers, suspect re-export shims — always trace `export *` / `import...export default` to the canonical repo-root files before trusting OR dismissing line-number evidence. The presence of a consumer GraphQL query is NOT sufficient for "real" — a consumer receiving [] that it always received is not being starved.

subject: GeneralMints → mint_event (Ruggy fix)
claim: ported ponder GeneralMints handler faithfully replicates envio mints.ts+vm-minted.ts AND emits live shadow/gif mints to NATS
ground_truth: FAGAN APPROVED + emit_path_correct: isLiveEvent→buildMintEnvelope→reorgSafeEmit→pending_emits(bera REORG_DEPTH 200)→OutboxFlushBera→publishEnvelope→NATS; 11 byte-parity tests pass; 83/83 vitest; ABI already had Transfer+Minted; ponder.config untouched; slugs mibera-shadow(Minted)/mibera-collection(gif Transfer)
refutation_attempted: FAGAN tried to break the emit path + envelope byte-parity; could not (publish runs unconditionally post-enrich, matches envio)
recommended_action: deploy on the coordinated fresh-green-boot + boundary→head backfill (tokens ~3242-3245+); restart Ruggy's bot once after
learning: a ported handler is graded the SAME way as a gap claim — the emit path + envelope byte-parity must be test/territory-verified, never asserted. This is the B-1 parity gate.

subject: BgtToken → forward handler
claim: ported ponder BgtToken handler faithfully replicates the envio source and writes the frozen entities forward
ground_truth:
FAITHFUL to envio bgt.ts + typecheck clean (tsc -p ponder-runtime/tsconfig.json --noEmit → exit 0, 0 lines). Every required check passes:

SUBSCRIPTION: ponder.on("BgtToken:QueueBoost", ...) registered (bgt.ts:66). Contract registered in ponder.config.mibera.ts:196 (BgtToken/BgtTokenAbi/berachain) — UNCHANGED. ABI event sig confirmed in abis/MiberaAbis.ts:41 ("event QueueBoost(address indexed acco
refutation_attempted: FAGAN line-by-line parity vs envio + tsc
recommended_action: deploy on the coordinated fresh-green-boot + boundary→head backfill
learning: handler-only port of a registered-but-unsubscribed contract — verified by FAGAN parity + combined tsc; rides the same boot

subject: CubBadges1155 → forward handler
claim: ported ponder CubBadges1155 handler faithfully replicates the envio source and writes the frozen entities forward
ground_truth:
FAITHFUL TO ENVIO + TYPECHECK CLEAN (tsc -p ponder-runtime/tsconfig.json --noEmit → exit 0).

Line-by-line parity vs envio src/handlers/badges1155.ts:

BOTH EVENTS SUBSCRIBED: ponder.on("CubBadges1155:TransferSingle") (handler L349) and ponder.on("CubBadges1155:TransferBatch") (handler L393). Erc1155Abi (abis/MiberaAbis.ts:46-47) declares both events with matching signatures; CubBadges1155 registe

subject: GeneralMints:Minted live-emit handler (Ruggy) — context.log runtime crash
claim: the FAGAN-approved general-mints handler processes a real on-chain GeneralMints:Minted (shadow) event and emits the mibera-shadow envelope
ground_truth: REFUTED-then-FIXED by the live cold-sync. green-v2 cold-sync hit GeneralMints:Minted tokenId 3242 @ bera block 21488420 and threw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'info')` at general-mints.ts:182 (context.log.info) -> uncaughtException -> crash-loop -> deployment CRASHED, cold-sync stalled 57%, is_ready never 1. Root cause: ponder 0.16.6 indexing context has no .log surface (bgt.ts:96 already flagged this; general-mints missed it). FAGAN + 83/83 vitest passed because the mocked context carried .log. Fixed in 879221ff (context.log.info/warn -> console.log/warn). Re-graded against territory: fresh build 06f4137f cold-synced past 21488420 cleanly; ponder_v2.mint_event advanced 3590->3594 @21:32:46; is_ready=1 @21:48:50, no crash.
refutation_attempted: live cold-sync replay over the real boundary->head range (construct-scar territory probe) — the event that crashed it now processes
recommended_action: FIXED + deployed; live shadow/gif mints will now create mint_event rows. NATS announce awaits a genuinely-live (non-historical) mint post-repoint (isLiveEvent gate).
learning: RLAI map!=territory, vindicated: a handler can pass FAGAN line-by-line parity AND its unit tests (mocked context) yet crash on the FIRST real runtime event. Grade ported handlers against a real cold-sync of the live boundary->head range, never just the diff + mocked tests. The context.log API mismatch is the canonical example.

subject: green-v2 gap-advance: bgt_boost_event / mint_event / badge_* / candies_*
claim: the 4 silent-gap handlers index forward past the boundary on green-v2 (vs frozen-only on green-v1)
ground_truth: PARTIAL confirm at is_ready=1. ponder_v2 vs green-v1 ponder.*: bgt_boost_event +1104 (1476071 vs 1474967) ADVANCED; mint_event +4 (3594 vs 3590) ADVANCED. badge_amount/badge_balance/badge_holder (CubBadges1155) and candies_backing/candies_inventory/mibera_order (CandiesMarket1155) FLAT (delta 0) — no on-chain events for those contracts in the boundary->head window. Handlers are subscribed (boot log: no 'No registered indexing functions' warning for them) and clean (no context.log bug class — grep-verified). 38 non-gap entities within tip-timing parity. pending_emits=0/dead_letter_emits=0 (isLiveEvent gates historical cold-sync emits — correct).
refutation_attempted: intra-vRR1 count diff ponder_v2 vs green-v1 at head; boot-log subscription check; context.log grep
recommended_action: bgt + GeneralMints proven; CubBadges1155 + CandiesMarket1155 await a real forward event to territory-confirm. Watch ponder_v2.{badge_holder,candies_backing} after a known badge transfer / candies trade, or coordinate a test event before/after the repoint.
learning: absence of forward rows on a low-frequency gap contract is NOT a handler failure — it's absence of events. Distinguish 'handler broken' from 'contract idle' by checking subscription + bug-class, and flag idle handlers as uncertain (not real) until the territory provides an event.

subject: encoded_traits wire-schema mismatch — Ruggy mibera-shadow announce
claim: the FAGAN-approved general-mints emit path produces an envelope the bot accepts + announces on a live Shadow mint
ground_truth: REFUTED-then-FIXED, LIVE-confirmed. @0xhoneyjar/events required encoded_traits ^0x[0-9a-f]+$ (hex); GeneralMints emits `traits` as a decimal-semicolon string ('25;10;78;...') -> bot rejected the live envelope payload-schema-invalid. The byte-parity test masked it (fake '0xdeadbeefcafe' fixture); the bot had only ever seen a synthetic envelope (sonar-api#50). Relaxed schema (loa-freeside 56585fd, PR#254) + repinned/redeployed bot (freeside-characters main d991ac5). Re-graded LIVE: token 3247 -> OUTBOX DRAINED -> bot 'mint routed' + '[announce-mint] posted' to #stonehenge, no schema reject. Root design gap: publishEnvelope ships without schema-validating (asymmetric enforcement) -> loa-freeside#255.
refutation_attempted: live on-chain mint replayed across the full producer->NATS->consumer path
recommended_action: Lever 1: publishEnvelope validates payload vs schema at publish time (loa-freeside#255). Secondary: bot announce is text-only — @0xhoneyjar/inventory module unavailable in the bot build (hasImage:false) — separate follow-up.
learning: A shared schema lib enforced ONLY on consume is false confidence: producer/consumer divergence stays invisible until a live message crosses the wire. Grade ports with a real on-chain event end-to-end, never fixtures + mocked context. 3rd map!=territory catch this session (after context.log + the gap-handler sweep).
